nuggets.headline: kendrick lamar is the first hip-hop artist to win a pulitzer prize
nuggets.text: Before Kendrick Lamar captivated the world with his headlining Super Bowl LIX halftime show, he made history in a different way. In 2018, he became the first hip-hop artist ever to be awarded the Pulitzer Prize for Music for his album *DAMN.*, the very album featuring "HUMBLE." This monumental achievement solidified his status not just as a music icon, but as a significant cultural voice. Lamar solidified his cultural icon status further by becoming the first solo hip-hop artist to headline the Super Bowl halftime show in 2025.

source.title: Kendrick Lamar brings America, 'Not Like Us' into history-making Super Bowl halftime show
source.quoteSnippet: As if there were any doubts of the Pulitzer Prize winning rapper's ability to put on a show.

nuggets.imageCaption: Kendrick Lamar, the Pulitzer-winning artist, performing on stage.

nuggets.headline: the iconic piano beat for kendrick lamar's "humble." was almost for gucci mane
nuggets.text: That driving piano riff you hear in "HUMBLE." was originally crafted by producer Mike WiLL Made-It with another artist in mind: Gucci Mane. It was the first track recorded for *DAMN.*, and initially, there was even talk of it appearing on Mike WiLL Made-It's own album. But Kendrick Lamar heard the beat and recognized its raw power, reminiscent of classic hip-hop tracks like Marley Marl's "The Symphony." He knew it was meant for his own record, and thankfully, others convinced him to keep it for *DAMN.*

source.title: Humble (song)
source.quoteSnippet: The beat for "Humble" was developed by Mike Will Made It, with the intention of recording the song with Gucci Mane. However, the song was later shown to Lamar. After recording, it was initially agreed upon that it would be released on Mike Will Made's debut album Ransom 2, but others convinced Lamar to keep it for his upcoming album.

nuggets.imageCaption: Kendrick Lamar in the striking music video for "HUMBLE."

nuggets.headline: if you love kendrick lamar, you need to hear jay rock
nuggets.text: If you're digging this Kendrick track, you absolutely have to check out Jay Rock. They’re not just collaborators, they’re part of the same crew, Black Hippy, alongside Ab-Soul and ScHoolboy Q. This means they came up together in the same West Coast hip-hop scene and have a deep, authentic connection that comes through in their shared projects and individual work.

source.title: Similar artists - Vince Staples | Last.fm
source.quoteSnippet: Quincey Matthew Hanley (born October 26, 1986), better known by his stage name ScHoolboy Q is an American recording hip hop artist from Los Angeles, California. Hanley is signed to Top Dawg Entertainment and is a member of Black Hippy along withJay Rock,Kendrick LamarandAb-Soul.

nuggets.imageCaption: Jay Rock, fellow member of the Black Hippy collective.

artistSummary: Kendrick Lamar, born in Compton, California, transcended his 'K.Dot' origins to become a Pulitzer Prize-winning rapper. He's known for his intricate lyricism and powerful storytelling, consistently pushing boundaries in hip-hop.
